title: 10 best herbal tinctures for red eyes
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for red eyes are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, which are used to alleviate redness and irritation in the eyes. These tinctures typically include ingredients such as calendula, eyebright, and goldenseal, all of which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe irritated eyes and reduce redness. By using herbal tinctures, individuals can find relief from eye strain, allergies, and other conditions that can cause redness and discomfort. For example, a person who spends long hours working on a computer may find that using an herbal tincture can help alleviate eye strain and reduce redness, improving their overall comfort and productivity. Similarly, someone who suffers from seasonal allergies may find that using an herbal tincture can help reduce eye redness and irritation, allowing them to enjoy the outdoors without discomfort. In this way, herbal tinctures can have a significant impact on people's lives, providing a natural and effective solution for red eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tincture helps with red eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can reduce redness and irritation. The herb, also known as Euphrasia officinalis, has been traditionally used to soothe eye-related issues, including bloodshot eyes and conjunctivitis. Its active compounds, such as flavonoids and tannins, can help to constrict blood vessels in the eyes, thereby reducing redness and promoting healthy circulation. Additionally, eyebright tincture has antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that can protect the eyes from environmental stressors and infections, further supporting eye health and reducing redness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tincture reduces inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from red eyes caused by irritation or inflammation. This property is important for red eyes as it helps to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing the appearance of redness. Active constituents include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and tannins.

Astringent: Eyebright tincture has astringent properties that help to tighten and tone the skin around the eyes, reducing redness and puffiness. This property is important for red eyes as it helps to improve the overall appearance of the eyes and reduce swelling. Active constituents include tannins and saponins.

Antimicrobial: Eyebright tincture has antimicrobial properties that help to protect the eyes from infection, which can cause redness and irritation. This property is important for red eyes as it helps to prevent the spread of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause eye infections. Active constituents include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and volatile oils.

Antioxidant: Eyebright tincture contains antioxidants that help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to redness and inflammation. This property is important for red eyes as it helps to reduce oxidative stress in the eyes and promote overall eye health. Active constituents include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and vitamins C and E.

Analgesic: Eyebright tincture has analgesic properties that help to relieve pain and discomfort associated with red eyes. This property is important for red eyes as it helps to alleviate symptoms such as burning, itching, and stinging. Active constituents include alkaloids and volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in bioflavonoids, the flowers of the eyebright plant can help reduce redness and irritation in the eyes.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, the leaves of the eyebright plant can help soothe inflammation and reduce redness in the eyes.
Stems: Containing tannins, the stems of the eyebright plant can help alleviate redness and irritation in the eyes.
Seeds: Packed with vitamins and minerals, the seeds of the eyebright plant can help improve eye health and reduce redness.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried eyebright herb and place it in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle in a cool, dark place for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rue tincture helps with red eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes. The herb's active compounds, including alkaloids and flavonoids, help to constrict blood vessels, which can alleviate redness and discomfort. Rue tincture can also help to stimulate the production of tears, which can help to keep the eyes lubricated and prevent dryness. Its antibacterial and antifungal properties can also help to protect the eyes from infection, making it a useful remedy for red eyes caused by various factors.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rue tincture can reduce inflammation in the eyes, which is important for red eyes as inflammation can cause blood vessels to dilate and become more visible. The active constituents responsible for this property include rutin, hesperidin, and quercetin.
Analgesic: Rue tincture has pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate the discomfort associated with red eyes. The active constituent responsible for this property is methyl nonyl ketone.

Antimicrobial: Rue tincture can help prevent and treat eye infections that can cause redness. The active constituents responsible for this property include methyl nonyl ketone, limonene, and phellandrene.
Antihistamine: Rue tincture can help alleviate allergic reactions that can cause red eyes. The active constituent responsible for this property is quercetin.
Astringent: Rue tincture can help reduce redness by constricting blood vessels in the eyes. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in bioflavonoids, the flowers of rue tincture plant can help reduce redness and irritation in eyes.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, the leaves of rue tincture plant can help soothe inflamed eyes and reduce redness.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, the seeds of rue tincture plant can help alleviate redness and irritation in eyes.
Buds: Packed with therapeutic compounds, the buds of rue tincture plant can help improve blood flow in the eyes and reduce redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rue herb, filling a clean jar twice with its leaves and stems, then chop finely.
Add 80 proof vodka to cover the chopped herb, ensuring a 1:2 herb-to-menstruum ratio.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the plant material.
Store the strained tincture in a dark glass bottle, keeping it in a cool, dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tincture helps with red eyes because it contains berberine, an alkaloid that has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. These properties can help reduce redness and irritation in the eyes by constricting blood vessels and tightening tissue, thereby reducing swelling and inflammation. Additionally, goldenseal has natural antimicrobial properties that can help fight off any potential infections that may be causing the redness. It is important to note that while goldenseal tincture can be an effective natural remedy for red eyes, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in cases of severe or persistent redness or discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Goldenseal tincture helps reduce red eyes by decreasing inflammation in the eyes, thanks to its active constituents like berberine and canadine.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of berberine and hydrastine, help combat eye infections causing redness.
Astringent: The astringent nature of goldenseal tincture, caused by tannins, helps to reduce red eyes by constricting blood vessels in the eyes.
Immune-boosting: Goldenseal tincture supports the immune system, which aids in preventing and fighting off eye infections that result in red eyes.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of goldenseal tincture, provided by its active constituents, help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, reducing redness and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the goldenseal tincture plant are used to make medicinal tea for red eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate irritation and reduce redness.
Roots: The roots of the goldenseal tincture plant are used in medicinal tea for red eyes because they contain berberine, an alkaloid known for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects.

Rhizomes: Goldenseal rhizomes are used in medicinal tea for red eyes due to their high concentration of isoquinoline alkaloids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

Flowers: The flowers of the goldenseal tincture plant are used in medicinal tea for red eyes because they contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried goldenseal root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 100-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the goldenseal, ensuring all pieces are covered.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the solid goldenseal material.
Store the finished goldenseal tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Neem tincture helps with red eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off any potential bacterial infection that may be causing the redness. Additionally, neem has been shown to improve blood circulation, which can help alleviate the redness and discomfort associated with irritated eyes. Its potent antioxidant properties can also help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further promoting eye health. Overall, neem tincture can provide a natural and effective solution for reducing red eyes and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Neem tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes by limiting the production of inflammatory substances like prostaglandins and histamines. This is largely due to the presence of nimbin, nimbidin, and quercetin.

Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help combat eye infections that may cause redness, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi. Key active constituents include azadirachtin, nimbin, and salannin.

Antioxidant: Neem tincture's antioxidant properties protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, which can lead to redness and irritation. Important antioxidants in neem include quercetin, gallic acid, and catechin.

Immune-modulating: The immune-modulating properties of neem tincture help balance the immune response in the eyes, reducing hypersensitivity and inflammation that can cause redness. Important constituents for this property include limonoids, flavonoids, and polysaccharides.
Astringent: Neem tincture's astringent properties help shrink blood vessels and reduce redness by constricting the dilated vessels in the eyes. This is primarily due to the tannins present in neem.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, neem leaves are commonly used to make medicinal tea for soothing red eyes.
Flowers: High in flavonoids, neem flowers help reduce inflammation and can be used in tea to alleviate red eyes.
Seeds: Neem seeds contain compounds that can help reduce redness and irritation in the eyes, making them ideal for medicinal tea.
Bark: The bark of the neem tree has anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in tea for treating red eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh neem leaves, rinsing them thoroughly; let them dry in a cool, dark place for 24 hours.
Chop or grind 2 cups of dried neem leaves, placing them in a clean, dry jar, filling it almost to the top.
Pour 100-proof vodka or rum (enough to cover the leaves) into the jar, sealing it tightly and shaking well.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the leaves; store the tincture in a dark glass bottle, using 20-30 drops for red eyes as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with red eyes because it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can alleviate irritation and reduce redness. The active compounds in chamomile, such as azulene and flavonoids, possess potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help decrease swelling and redness in the eyes. Additionally, chamomile has antimicrobial properties that can help protect the eyes from potential infections, further contributing to its ability to soothe red eyes. Its calming nature helps relax the eye muscles, reducing strain and alleviating discomfort. Overall, german chamomile tincture is a natural and effective remedy for red eyes, providing quick relief and promoting eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for red eyes as inflammation can cause blood vessels in the eye to expand and become more visible. The active constituent responsible for this property is the terpene azulene.
Antimicrobial: It helps prevent and treat eye infections, which can cause redness. The active constituents responsible for this property include the flavonoids apigenin and luteolin.

Analgesic: German chamomile tincture alleviates pain, which can be a symptom of red eyes caused by conditions like conjunctivitis. The active constituent responsible for this property is the sesquiterpene α-bisabolol.
Antioxidant: It protects the eyes from oxidative stress, which can contribute to red eyes. The active constituents responsible for this property include the flavonoids apigenin and luteolin.
Vasoconstrictor: German chamomile tincture helps constrict blood vessels in the eye, reducing redness. The active constituent responsible for this property is the flavonoid apigenin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile flowers help soothe irritated eyes and reduce redness.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and terpenoids, chamomile leaves can help alleviate red eyes by reducing inflammation and irritation.
Seeds: The seeds of German chamomile contain essential oils that can help reduce redness and inflammation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the chopped flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solids.
Store the final tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle in a cool, dark place for up to a year, using 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) as needed for red eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with red eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce redness and irritation in the eyes. The herb's active compounds, such as allantoin, aid in soothing and healing inflamed tissues, while its astringent properties help to tighten and protect the delicate skin around the eyes. Additionally, plantain tincture has been found to alleviate symptoms of conjunctivitis, an infection that commonly causes red, itchy, and watery eyes. By promoting overall eye health and providing relief from discomfort, plantain tincture serves as a natural and effective remedy for red eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture helps reduce redness and inflammation in the eyes by inhibiting the production of inflammatory compounds, thanks to its active constituents, such as flavonoids, mucilage, and tannins.
Astringent: The astringent property of plantain tincture helps tighten and soothe irritated eye tissues, reducing redness and discomfort, due to the presence of tannins and mucilage.
Antimicrobial: Plantain tincture has antimicrobial properties that help combat eye infections causing redness, as it contains active constituents like allantoin, flavonoids, and saponins.

Antihistamine: Plantain tincture can alleviate allergic reactions causing red eyes, as it contains antihistamine properties that help inhibit the release of histamine, responsible for allergic responses.
Demulcent: The soothing, demulcent property of plantain tincture helps protect and lubricate the eyes, reducing redness and irritation, due to the presence of mucilage and tannins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the plantain tincture plant are used to make medicinal tea for red eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help soothe irritated eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of the plantain tincture plant are rich in mucilage, making them an effective demulcent that can help relieve red eyes by reducing inflammation and providing a soothing effect.

Flowers: The flowers of the plantain tincture plant contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for making medicinal tea to alleviate red eyes and reduce irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest plantain leaves (Plantago major) from a clean, pesticide-free environment, ensuring they are fully grown and healthy.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of fresh leaves and place them in a clean, dry glass jar, then pour 16 ounces of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the leaves to cover them completely.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the specified time, strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid from the leaves.

Store the final herbal plantain tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to 2 years, using 1-2 droppersful (20-40 drops) in water or directly under the tongue, as needed for red eye relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with red eyes because it contains anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can alleviate irritation and reduce redness in the eyes. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, have been shown to improve blood flow and promote healing in the eye area. Moreover, pot marigold tincture has natural antimicrobial and antiseptic properties that can help protect the eyes from infection and inflammation, further reducing redness and discomfort. Overall, pot marigold tincture is a safe and effective natural remedy for red eyes, providing quick relief and long-term benefits for eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce red eyes by decreasing inflammation in the eye, thanks to its active constituents, including flavonoids and carotenoids.
Antimicrobial: The tincture's ability to fight microbial infections helps prevent red eyes caused by bacterial or fungal infections, due to its active compound, lutein.
Antioxidant: Pot marigold tincture protects the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, reducing redness and irritation, due to the presence of antioxidants like vitamin E and carotenoids.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help tighten and soothe irritated eye tissues, reducing redness and discomfort, due to the tannins present in pot marigold.
Analgesic: Pot marigold tincture alleviates pain and discomfort associated with red eyes, thanks to its active constituents, such as flavonoids and triterpenes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The pot marigold flowers are rich in flavonoids and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate red eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and irritation in the eyes.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are a good source of essential fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation and redness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 handfuls of dried pot marigold flowers and place them in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 750ml of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the flowers until they are completely submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the allotted time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or muslin into a clean, dry glass bottle, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Label the bottle with the name, ingredients, and date, and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Use 1-2 droppersful in a small amount of water for red eyes as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tincture helps with red eyes because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce redness and irritation in the eyes. The active compounds in the tincture, such as ginkgolides and bilobalide, help to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels surrounding the eyes. This can help to alleviate redness, puffiness, and discomfort in the eyes, leaving them looking and feeling refreshed and rejuvenated. Additionally, the tincture can help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors, further supporting eye health and reducing the risk of redness and irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maidenhair tree tincture helps reduce red eyes by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its active constituents, mainly glycosides and flavonoids.
Astringent: Its astringent properties, due to tannins, help tighten and soothe irritated eye tissues, reducing redness.
Antioxidant: Maidenhair tree tincture contains powerful antioxidants, such as triterpenes, that protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which can cause redness.
Antihistamine: Maidenhair tree tincture may help alleviate red eyes by inhibiting histamine release, providing relief from allergic reactions.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of maidenhair tree tincture, attributed to its essential oils, help in reducing eye pain and discomfort associated with red eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the maidenhair tree are commonly used in making medicinal tea for red eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that can help alleviate redness and irritation in the eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of the maidenhair tree are rich in antioxidants and can help reduce inflammation, making them a useful addition to medicinal tea for red eyes.
Bark: The bark of the maidenhair tree contains tannins that can help soothe irritated eyes and reduce redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the maidenhair tree (Ginkgo biloba) leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and healthy, then rinse them thoroughly.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of dried leaves and place them in a clean, dry jar, filling it up to three-quarters capacity.
Pour 16 ounces of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the ground leaves, ensuring they are fully submerged, then seal the jar tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently once a day.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid, and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butcher's broom tincture helps with red eyes because it contains ruscogenins, which have anti-inflammatory and vasoconstrictive properties. These properties can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes by constricting the blood vessels and decreasing the flow of blood to the area. Additionally, butcher's broom has been shown to improve circulation and reduce fluid buildup, which can also contribute to red eyes. Its anti-inflammatory effects may also help alleviate any discomfort or irritation associated with red eyes. Overall, butcher's broom tincture can provide relief for red eyes by addressing the underlying causes of the redness and reducing inflammation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Butcher's broom tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for red eyes as it helps alleviate irritation and swelling. This property is attributed to the ruscogenins and neoruscogenins in butcher's broom.

Venotonic: It strengthens blood vessel walls and improves circulation, which is beneficial for red eyes since it can reduce redness caused by broken or dilated blood vessels. The saponins, such as ruscogenins, are responsible for this effect.

Antioxidant: Butcher's broom tincture has antioxidant properties that help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage. This is important for red eyes as it can alleviate inflammation and promote overall eye health. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in butcher's broom contribute to its antioxidant properties.

Analgesic: Butcher's broom tincture has pain-relieving properties, which can be helpful for red eyes that are caused by discomfort or pain. This property is attributed to the steroidal saponins, such as ruscogenins, in butcher's broom.

Antimicrobial: Butcher's broom tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent or treat eye infections, which can lead to red eyes. This property is important for red eyes as it can help address the underlying cause of redness. The tannins and essential oils in butcher's broom contribute to its antimicrobial properties.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The underground stem of butcher's broom contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate red eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of butcher's broom contain ruscogenins, which have been shown to reduce redness and irritation in the eyes.
Bark: The bark of butcher's broom contains active compounds that can improve blood flow to the eyes, reducing redness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried butcher's broom herb into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the solid plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL (20-40 drops) up to three times a day as needed for red eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tincture helps with red eyes because it contains properties that can reduce inflammation and irritation. This herbal extract has astringent and soothing effects, which can help shrink swollen blood vessels in the eyes and alleviate redness. Its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties can also help protect the eyes from damage caused by environmental stressors, further reducing redness and promoting overall eye health. By using witch hazel tincture, you can naturally soothe and relieve red eyes, providing a gentle and effective solution for this common issue.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling in eyes caused by irritation or inflammation, thanks to the active constituents tannins and volatile oils.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help tighten and soothe irritated skin around the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort, due to the high tannin content.
Antioxidant: Witch hazel tincture's antioxidant properties protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, contributing to overall eye health, as a result of its polyphenol and flavonoid content.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of witch hazel tincture help prevent eye infections that can cause redness and other symptoms, owing to the presence of volatile oils and tannins.

Vasoconstrictor: Witch hazel tincture's vasoconstrictor properties help narrow blood vessels in the eyes, reducing redness caused by dilated blood vessels, due to the presence of gallic acid and other active constituents.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the witch hazel tincture plant contains tannins that can help reduce inflammation and soothe red eyes.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant have astringent properties that can help alleviate redness and irritation in the eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of the witch hazel plant contain antioxidants that can help reduce redness and promote healthy eyes.
Buds: The buds of the plant are rich in oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for reducing redness and irritation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried witch hazel bark into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 16 ounces of vodka or grain alcohol over the chopped bark, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, squeezing out excess liquid.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 1-2 drops in each eye as needed for redness relief.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat red eyes with herbal tinctures is by using eyebright (Euphrasia officinalis) and goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis). Eyebright, a flowering plant, has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help alleviate redness and irritation. Goldenseal, a perennial herb, contains berberine, which has been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. Mix 1-2 drops of each tincture in a cup of water and use as an eyewash up to three times a day. Consult a healthcare professional before use if you have a medical condition or are taking medication.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to red eyes that are treated with herbal tinctures include dry eye, eye strain, and conjunctivitis. Eyebright tincture is commonly used to soothe red and irritated eyes, while goldenseal and calendula tinctures have antibacterial properties that can help treat conjunctivitis. For dry eye and eye strain, chamomile and fennel tinctures can help reduce inflammation and relieve discomfort. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal tinctures to treat eye ailments, as some herbs can interact with medications or exacerbate certain medical conditions.
